question: Show the names of countries and their official languages.
sql:
SELECT languages.name, countries.name
FROM official_languages
JOIN languages ON official_languages.language_id = languages.id
JOIN countries ON official_languages.country_id = countries.id

question: What are the names of the countries, as well as the names of their official langauges?
sql:
SELECT languages.name, countries.name
FROM languages
JOIN official_languages ON official_languages.language_id = languages.id
JOIN countries ON official_languages.country_id = countries.id

question: Show the official languages and the number of countries speaking each language.

joins: official_languages JOIN languages ON official_languages.language_id = languages.id

sql:
SELECT languages.name, COUNT(official_languages.country_id) 
FROM official_languages 
JOIN languages ON official_languages.language_id = languages.id 
GROUP BY languages.name 
ORDER BY languages.name ASC

question: What are the names of the different official languages, as well as the number of countries that speak each?

joins: languages JOIN official_languages ON languages.id = official_languages.language_id
aggregations: COUNT(official_languages.country_id) AS country_count GROUP BY languages.name

sql:
SELECT languages.name, COUNT(official_languages.country_id) AS country_count
FROM languages
JOIN official_languages ON languages.id = official_languages.language_id
GROUP BY languages.name
ORDER BY languages.name

question: Show the official language spoken by the most number of countries.

joins: official_languages JOIN languages ON official_languages.language_id = languages.id
aggregations: COUNT(official_languages.country_id) AS country_count GROUP BY languages.name

sql:
SELECT languages.name, COUNT(official_languages.country_id) AS country_count
FROM official_languages
JOIN languages ON official_languages.language_id = languages.id
GROUP BY languages.name
ORDER BY country_count DESC
LIMIT 1

question: What is the official language that is most common?
sql:
SELECT languages.name
FROM languages
JOIN official_languages ON official_languages.language_id = languages.id
JOIN countries ON official_languages.country_id = countries.id
GROUP BY languages.name
ORDER BY COUNT(official_languages.language_id) DESC
LIMIT 1

question: Show the official languages spoken by at least two countries.

joins: official_languages JOIN languages ON official_languages.language_id = languages.id

sql:
SELECT languages.name
FROM official_languages
JOIN languages ON official_languages.language_id = languages.id
GROUP BY languages.name
HAVING COUNT(official_languages.country_id) >= 2

question: Which official languages are spoken in two or more countries?

joins: languages JOIN official_languages ON languages.id = official_languages.language_id

sql:
SELECT languages.name
FROM languages
JOIN official_languages ON languages.id = official_languages.language_id
GROUP BY languages.name
HAVING COUNT(official_languages.country_id) >= 2

question: Show the average overall scores of countries whose official language is "English".
sql:
SELECT AVG(countries.overall_score)
FROM languages
JOIN official_languages ON official_languages.language_id = languages.id
JOIN countries ON official_languages.country_id = countries.id
WHERE languages.name = 'English'

question: What is the average overall score across countries with English as their official language?
sql:
SELECT AVG(countries.overall_score)
FROM languages
JOIN official_languages ON official_languages.language_id = languages.id
JOIN countries ON official_languages.country_id = countries.id
WHERE languages.name = 'English'

sql:

JOIN languages ON official_languages.language_id = languages.id

question: Show the official languages sorted in descending order by the average overall scores among countries speaking them.
sql:

FROM languages

JOIN countries ON official_languages.country_id = countries.id

question: What are the names of the official languages, sorted descending by the average overall scores across the countries that correspond to each?

JOIN countries ON official_languages.country_id = countries.id

official_languages JOIN countries ON official_languages.country_id = countries.id

question: Show the names of languages that are the official language for both countries with overall score greater than 95 and countries with overall score less than than 90.
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT l.name
FROM languages l
JOIN official_languages ol ON l.id = ol.language_id
JOIN countries c ON ol.country_id = c.id
WHERE c.overall_score > 95
INTERSECT
SELECT DISTINCT l.name
FROM languages l
JOIN official_languages ol ON l.id = ol.language_id
JOIN countries c ON ol.country_id = c.id
WHERE c.overall_score < 90

question: What are the names of languages that are the official language not only for countries that have an overall score of above 95, but also for countries that have an overall score below 90?
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT l.name
FROM languages l
JOIN official_languages ol ON l.id = ol.language_id
JOIN countries c ON ol.country_id = c.id
WHERE c.overall_score > 95
INTERSECT
SELECT DISTINCT l.name
FROM languages l
JOIN official_languages ol ON l.id = ol.language_id
JOIN countries c ON ol.country_id = c.id
WHERE c.overall_score < 90

question: List the average room count of the properties with gardens.

FROM Properties

question: What are the ids of users who have searched at least twice, and what did they search?

joins: User_Searches JOIN Users ON User_Searches.user_id = Users.user_id
aggregations: GROUP BY User_Searches.user_id, User_Searches.search_string HAVING COUNT(User_Searches.search_seq) >= 2

sql:
SELECT User_Searches.user_id, User_Searches.search_string
FROM User_Searches
JOIN Users ON User_Searches.user_id = Users.user_id
GROUP BY User_Searches.user_id, User_Searches.search_string
HAVING COUNT(User_Searches.search_seq) >= 2

question: Return the ids of users who have performed two or more searches, as well as their search sequence.
sql:
SELECT User_Searches.user_id, User_Searches.search_seq
FROM User_Searches
GROUP BY User_Searches.user_id
HAVING COUNT(User_Searches.search_seq) >= 2
ORDER BY User_Searches.user_id, User_Searches.search_seq

question: When was the time of the latest search by a user?
sql: SELECT MAX(search_datetime) FROM User_Searches

question: What was the time of the most recent search?
sql: SELECT User_Searches.search_datetime FROM User_Searches ORDER BY User_Searches.search_datetime DESC LIMIT 1

question: What are all the user searches time and content? Sort the result descending by content.
sql:

FROM User_Searches

question: Return the search strings and corresonding time stamps for all user searches, sorted by search string descending.
sql: SELECT search_string, search_datetime FROM User_Searches ORDER BY search_string DESC

question: What are the zip codes of properties which do not belong to users who own at most 2 properties?

joins: Properties JOIN Addresses ON Properties.property_address_id = Addresses.address_id
Properties JOIN Users ON Properties.owner_user_id = Users.user_id
conditions: Users.user_id NOT IN (SELECT owner_user_id FROM Properties GROUP BY owner_user_id HAVING COUNT(property_id) <= 2)

sql:
SELECT Addresses.zip_postcode
FROM Properties
JOIN Addresses ON Properties.property_address_id = Addresses.address_id
JOIN Users ON Properties.owner_user_id = Users.user_id
WHERE Users.user_id NOT IN (
    SELECT owner_user_id
    FROM Properties
    GROUP BY owner_user_id
    HAVING COUNT(property_id) <= 2
)
GROUP BY Addresses.zip_postcode

question: Return the zip codes for properties not belonging to users who own two or fewer properties.

joins: Properties JOIN Addresses ON Properties.property_address_id = Addresses.address_id
Properties JOIN Users ON Properties.owner_user_id = Users.user_id
conditions: Users.user_id NOT IN (SELECT owner_user_id FROM Properties GROUP BY owner_user_id HAVING COUNT(property_id) <= 2)

sql:
SELECT DISTINCT Addresses.zip_postcode
FROM Properties
JOIN Addresses ON Properties.property_address_id = Addresses.address_id
JOIN Users ON Properties.owner_user_id = Users.user_id
WHERE Users.user_id NOT IN (
    SELECT owner_user_id
    FROM Properties
    GROUP BY owner_user_id
    HAVING COUNT(property_id) <= 2
)

question: Where do the Senior Citizens live? List building, street, and the city.

joins: Users JOIN Addresses ON Users.user_address_id = Addresses.address_id
conditions: Users.age_category_code IN (SELECT age_category_code FROM Ref_Age_Categories WHERE age_category_description LIKE '%Senior%')

sql:
SELECT Addresses.line_1_number_building, Addresses.line_2_number_street, Addresses.town_city
FROM Users
JOIN Addresses ON Users.user_address_id = Addresses.address_id
WHERE Users.age_category_code IN (SELECT age_category_code FROM Ref_Age_Categories WHERE age_category_description LIKE '%Senior%')

question: What are the buildings, streets, and cities corresponding to the addresses of senior citizens?

joins: Users JOIN Addresses ON Users.user_address_id = Addresses.address_id
conditions: Users.age_category_code IN (SELECT age_category_code FROM Ref_Age_Categories WHERE age_category_description LIKE '%senior%' OR age_category_description LIKE '%Senior%')

sql:
SELECT Addresses.line_1_number_building, Addresses.line_2_number_street, Addresses.town_city
FROM Users
JOIN Addresses ON Users.user_address_id = Addresses.address_id
WHERE Users.age_category_code IN (
    SELECT age_category_code 
    FROM Ref_Age_Categories 
    WHERE age_category_description LIKE '%senior%' OR age_category_description LIKE '%Senior%'
)

question: How many photos does each owner has of his or her properties? List user id and number of photos.

question: What is the total max price of the properties owned by single mothers or students?
sql:
SELECT SUM(Properties.price_max)
FROM Users
JOIN Properties ON Properties.owner_user_id = Users.user_id
WHERE Users.user_category_code IN ('single_mother', 'student')

question: Give the total max price corresponding to any properties owned by single mothers or students.
sql:
SELECT MAX(Properties.price_max)
FROM Users
JOIN Properties ON Properties.owner_user_id = Users.user_id
WHERE Users.user_category_code IN ('single_mother', 'student')

question: What are the date stamps and property names for each item of property history, ordered by date stamp?

joins: User_Property_History JOIN Properties ON User_Property_History.property_id = Properties.property_id

sql:
SELECT User_Property_History.datestamp, Properties.property_name
FROM User_Property_History
JOIN Properties ON User_Property_History.property_id = Properties.property_id
ORDER BY User_Property_History.datestamp

question: Return the date stamp and property name for each property history event, sorted by date stamp.
sql:
SELECT User_Property_History.datestamp, Properties.property_name
FROM User_Property_History
JOIN Properties ON User_Property_History.property_id = Properties.property_id
ORDER BY User_Property_History.datestamp

question: What is the description of the most common property type? List the description and code.

joins: Properties JOIN Ref_Property_Types ON Properties.property_type_code = Ref_Property_Types.property_type_code

sql:
SELECT Ref_Property_Types.property_type_description, Ref_Property_Types.property_type_code
FROM Properties
JOIN Ref_Property_Types ON Properties.property_type_code = Ref_Property_Types.property_type_code
GROUP BY Ref_Property_Types.property_type_code, Ref_Property_Types.property_type_description
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1

question: What is the most common property type, and what is its description.

joins: Properties JOIN Ref_Property_Types ON Properties.property_type_code = Ref_Property_Types.property_type_code
aggregations: COUNT(Properties.property_id) AS property_count GROUP BY Ref_Property_Types.property_type_code, Ref_Property_Types.property_type_description

sql:
SELECT Ref_Property_Types.property_type_code, Ref_Property_Types.property_type_description
FROM Properties
JOIN Ref_Property_Types ON Properties.property_type_code = Ref_Property_Types.property_type_code
GROUP BY Ref_Property_Types.property_type_code, Ref_Property_Types.property_type_description
ORDER BY COUNT(Properties.property_id) DESC
LIMIT 1

question: What is the detailed description of the age category code 'Over 60'?
sql:
SELECT Ref_Age_Categories.age_category_description
FROM Ref_Age_Categories
WHERE Ref_Age_Categories.age_category_code = 'Over 60'

question: Give the category description of the age category 'Over 60'.
sql: SELECT age_category_description FROM Ref_Age_Categories WHERE age_category_code = 'Over 60'

question: What are the different room sizes, and how many of each are there?
sql: SELECT room_size, COUNT(room_size) AS count FROM Rooms GROUP BY room_size

question: Return the number of rooms with each different room size.

aggregations: COUNT(*) AS room_count GROUP BY Rooms.room_size

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS room_count, room_size FROM Rooms GROUP BY room_size

question: In which country does the user with first name Robbie live?
sql:
SELECT Addresses.country
FROM Users
JOIN Addresses ON Users.user_address_id = Addresses.address_id
WHERE Users.first_name = 'Robbie'

question: Return the country in which the user with first name Robbie lives.
sql:
SELECT Addresses.country
FROM Users
JOIN Addresses ON Users.user_address_id = Addresses.address_id
WHERE Users.first_name = 'Robbie'

question: What are the first, middle and last names of users who own the property they live in?
sql:
SELECT Users.first_name, Users.middle_name, Users.last_name
FROM Users
JOIN Properties ON Users.user_id = Properties.owner_user_id

question: Return the full names of users who live in properties that they own.
sql:
SELECT Users.first_name, Users.middle_name, Users.last_name
FROM Users
JOIN Properties ON Users.user_id = Properties.owner_user_id

question: List the search content of the users who do not own a single property.

joins: Users JOIN User_Searches ON Users.user_id = User_Searches.user_id
Users LEFT JOIN Properties ON Users.user_id = Properties.owner_user_id

sql:
SELECT User_Searches.search_string
FROM Users
JOIN User_Searches ON Users.user_id = User_Searches.user_id
LEFT JOIN Properties ON Users.user_id = Properties.owner_user_id
WHERE Properties.owner_user_id IS NULL

question: What search strings were entered by users who do not own any properties?

joins: Users JOIN User_Searches ON Users.user_id = User_Searches.user_id
Users LEFT JOIN Properties ON Users.user_id = Properties.owner_user_id

sql:
SELECT User_Searches.search_string
FROM Users
JOIN User_Searches ON Users.user_id = User_Searches.user_id
LEFT JOIN Properties ON Users.user_id = Properties.owner_user_id
WHERE Properties.owner_user_id IS NULL

question: List the last names and ids of users who have at least 2 properties and searched at most twice.
sql:
SELECT Users.last_name, Users.user_id
FROM Users
JOIN Properties ON Users.user_id = Properties.owner_user_id
JOIN User_Searches ON Users.user_id = User_Searches.user_id
GROUP BY Users.user_id, Users.last_name
HAVING COUNT(Properties.property_id) >= 2 AND COUNT(User_Searches.search_seq) <= 2

question: What are the last names and ids of users who have searched two or fewer times, and own two or more properties?
sql:
SELECT Users.last_name, Users.user_id
FROM Users
JOIN User_Searches ON Users.user_id = User_Searches.user_id
JOIN Properties ON Users.user_id = Properties.owner_user_id
GROUP BY Users.user_id, Users.last_name
HAVING COUNT(User_Searches.search_seq) <= 2 AND COUNT(Properties.property_id) >= 2

question: How many bikes are heavier than 780 grams?
sql: SELECT COUNT(bike.weight) FROM bike WHERE bike.weight > 780

question: List the product names and weights of the bikes in ascending order of price.
sql:
SELECT bike.product_name, bike.weight
FROM bike
ORDER BY bike.price ASC

question: List the heat, name, and nation for all the cyclists.
sql: SELECT heat, name, nation FROM cyclist

question: What are the maximum and minimum weight of all bikes?
sql: SELECT MAX(weight) AS max_weight, MIN(weight) AS min_weight FROM bike

question: What is the average price of the bikes made of material 'Carbon CC'?
sql: SELECT AVG(bike.price) FROM bike WHERE bike.material = 'Carbon CC'

question: What are the name and result of the cyclists not from 'Russia' ?
sql: SELECT cyclist.name, cyclist.result FROM cyclist WHERE cyclist.nation != 'Russia'

question: What are the distinct ids and product names of the bikes that are purchased after year 2015?
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT bike.id, bike.product_name
FROM bike
JOIN cyclists_own_bikes ON bike.id = cyclists_own_bikes.bike_id
WHERE cyclists_own_bikes.purchase_year > 2015

question: What are the ids and names of racing bikes that are purchased by at least 4 cyclists?

aggregations: GROUP BY bike.id, bike.product_name HAVING COUNT(cyclists_own_bikes.cyclist_id) >= 4

sql:
SELECT bike.id, bike.product_name
FROM bike
JOIN cyclists_own_bikes ON bike.id = cyclists_own_bikes.bike_id
GROUP BY bike.id, bike.product_name
HAVING COUNT(cyclists_own_bikes.cyclist_id) >= 4

question: What are the id and name of the cyclist who owns the most bikes?
sql:
SELECT cyclist.id, cyclist.name
FROM cyclist
JOIN cyclists_own_bikes ON cyclist.id = cyclists_own_bikes.cyclist_id
GROUP BY cyclist.id, cyclist.name
ORDER BY COUNT(cyclists_own_bikes.bike_id) DESC
LIMIT 1

question: What are the distinct product names of bikes owned by cyclists from 'Russia' or cyclists from 'Great Britain'?
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT bike.product_name
FROM bike
JOIN cyclists_own_bikes ON cyclists_own_bikes.bike_id = bike.id
JOIN cyclist ON cyclists_own_bikes.cyclist_id = cyclist.id
WHERE cyclist.nation = 'Russia' OR cyclist.nation = 'Great Britain'

question: How many different levels of heat are there for the cyclists?
sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT heat) FROM cyclist

question: How many cyclists did not purchase any bike after year 2015?

joins: cyclists_own_bikes JOIN cyclist ON cyclists_own_bikes.cyclist_id = cyclist.id

sql:
SELECT COUNT(cyclist.id)
FROM cyclist
LEFT JOIN cyclists_own_bikes ON cyclists_own_bikes.cyclist_id = cyclist.id
WHERE cyclists_own_bikes.purchase_year <= 2015 OR cyclists_own_bikes.purchase_year IS NULL

question: What are the names of distinct racing bikes that are purchased by the cyclists with better results than '4:21.558' ?
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT bike.product_name
FROM bike
JOIN cyclists_own_bikes ON cyclists_own_bikes.bike_id = bike.id
JOIN cyclist ON cyclists_own_bikes.cyclist_id = cyclist.id
WHERE cyclist.result > 4.358

question: List the name and price of the bike that is owned by both the cyclists named 'Bradley Wiggins' and the cyclist named 'Antonio Tauler'.

joins: cyclists_own_bikes JOIN cyclist ON cyclists_own_bikes.cyclist_id = cyclist.id
cyclists_own_bikes JOIN bike ON cyclists_own_bikes.bike_id = bike.id

sql:
SELECT DISTINCT b.product_name, b.price
FROM bike b
JOIN cyclists_own_bikes cob ON b.id = cob.bike_id
JOIN cyclist c ON cob.cyclist_id = c.id
WHERE c.name = 'Bradley Wiggins'
AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM cyclists_own_bikes cob2
    JOIN cyclist c2 ON cob2.cyclist_id = c2.id
    WHERE cob2.bike_id = b.id
    AND c2.name = 'Antonio Tauler'
)

question: Show the name, nation and result for the cyclists who did not purchase any racing bike.

joins: cyclist LEFT JOIN cyclists_own_bikes ON cyclist.id = cyclists_own_bikes.cyclist_id

sql:
SELECT cyclist.name, cyclist.nation, cyclist.result
FROM cyclist
LEFT JOIN cyclists_own_bikes ON cyclist.id = cyclists_own_bikes.cyclist_id
WHERE cyclists_own_bikes.cyclist_id IS NULL

question: What are the names of the bikes that have substring 'fiber' in their material?
sql: SELECT bike.product_name FROM bike WHERE bike.material LIKE '%fiber%'

question: How many bikes does each cyclist own? Order by cyclist id.

aggregations: COUNT(cyclists_own_bikes.bike_id) AS bike_count GROUP BY cyclist.id

sql:
SELECT cyclist.id, COUNT(cyclists_own_bikes.bike_id) AS bike_count
FROM cyclist
JOIN cyclists_own_bikes ON cyclist.id = cyclists_own_bikes.cyclist_id
GROUP BY cyclist.id
ORDER BY cyclist.id

question: What are the ids with apple flavor?

question: Give the ids of goods that cost less than 3 dollars.

question: List the distinct ids of all customers who bought a cake with lemon flavor?
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT customers.Id
FROM customers
JOIN receipts ON receipts.CustomerId = customers.Id
JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id
WHERE goods.Flavor = 'lemon' AND goods.Food = 'cake'

question: What are the distinct ids of customers who bought lemon flavored cake?
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT customers.Id
FROM customers
JOIN receipts ON receipts.CustomerId = customers.Id
JOIN items ON items.Receipt = receipts.ReceiptNumber
JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id
WHERE goods.Flavor = 'lemon'

question: For each type of food, tell me how many customers have ever bought it.
sql:
SELECT COUNT(receipts.CustomerId) AS customer_count, goods.Food
FROM goods
JOIN items ON items.Item = goods.Id
JOIN receipts ON items.Receipt = receipts.ReceiptNumber
JOIN customers ON receipts.CustomerId = customers.Id
GROUP BY goods.Food

question: How many customers have bought each food?

aggregations: COUNT(*) AS customer_count GROUP BY goods.Food

sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS customer_count, goods.Food
FROM goods
JOIN items ON items.Item = goods.Id
JOIN receipts ON items.Receipt = receipts.ReceiptNumber
JOIN customers ON receipts.CustomerId = customers.Id
GROUP BY goods.Food

question: Find the id of customers who shopped at the bakery at least 15 times.
sql:
SELECT customers.Id
FROM customers
JOIN receipts ON customers.Id = receipts.CustomerId
JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
GROUP BY customers.Id
HAVING COUNT(receipts.ReceiptNumber) >= 15

question: What are the customer ids of customers who have at least 15 receipts?
sql:
SELECT customers.Id
FROM customers
JOIN receipts ON receipts.CustomerId = customers.Id
GROUP BY receipts.CustomerId
HAVING COUNT(receipts.ReceiptNumber) >= 15

question: What is the last name of the customers who shopped at the bakery more than 10 times?
sql:
SELECT customers.LastName
FROM customers
JOIN receipts ON customers.Id = receipts.CustomerId
JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
GROUP BY customers.LastName
HAVING COUNT(receipts.ReceiptNumber) > 10

question: Give the last names of customers who have been to the bakery more than 10 times?
sql:
SELECT customers.LastName
FROM customers
JOIN receipts ON customers.Id = receipts.CustomerId
GROUP BY customers.Id, customers.LastName
HAVING COUNT(receipts.ReceiptNumber) > 10

question: How many types of Cake does this bakery sell?
sql: SELECT COUNT(goods.Food) FROM goods WHERE goods.Food = 'Cake'

question: Count the number of types of cake this bakery sells.
sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Flavor) FROM goods WHERE Food = 'cake'

question: List all the flavors of Croissant available in this bakery.
sql: SELECT Flavor FROM goods WHERE Food = 'Croissant'

question: What are all the flavors of croissant?
sql: SELECT DISTINCT Flavor FROM goods WHERE Food = 'croissant'

question: Give me a list of all the distinct items bought by the customer number 15.
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT g.*
FROM customers c
JOIN receipts r ON c.Id = r.CustomerId
JOIN items i ON r.ReceiptNumber = i.Receipt
JOIN goods g ON i.Item = g.Id
WHERE c.Id = 15

question: What are all the distinct items bought by customer 15?
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT g.*
FROM customers c
JOIN receipts r ON c.Id = r.CustomerId
JOIN items i ON r.ReceiptNumber = i.Receipt
JOIN goods g ON i.Item = g.Id
WHERE c.Id = 15

question: For each type of food, what are the average, maximum and minimum price?
sql:
SELECT goods.Food, AVG(goods.Price) AS avg_price, MAX(goods.Price) AS max_price, MIN(goods.Price) AS min_price
FROM goods
GROUP BY goods.Food

question: What are the average, minimum and maximum prices for each food?
sql:
SELECT goods.Food, AVG(goods.Price) AS avg_price, MIN(goods.Price) AS min_price, MAX(goods.Price) AS max_price
FROM goods
GROUP BY goods.Food

question: Find the receipt numbers where both Cake and Cookie were bought.
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT i1.Receipt
FROM items i1
JOIN goods g1 ON i1.Item = g1.Id
JOIN receipts r1 ON i1.Receipt = r1.ReceiptNumber
WHERE g1.Food = 'Cake'
AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM items i2
    JOIN goods g2 ON i2.Item = g2.Id
    WHERE i2.Receipt = i1.Receipt
    AND g2.Food = 'Cookie'
)

question: What are the receipt numbers for instances where both cakes and cookies were purchased?
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT i1.Receipt
FROM items i1
JOIN goods g1 ON i1.Item = g1.Id
JOIN receipts r1 ON i1.Receipt = r1.ReceiptNumber
WHERE g1.Food = 'cake'
AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM items i2
    JOIN goods g2 ON i2.Item = g2.Id
    WHERE i2.Receipt = i1.Receipt
    AND g2.Food = 'cookie'
)

question: Find all the receipt numbers in which customer with last name LOGAN purchased Croissant.

joins: customers JOIN receipts ON customers.Id = receipts.CustomerId
receipts JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
items JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id

sql:
SELECT receipts.ReceiptNumber
FROM customers
JOIN receipts ON customers.Id = receipts.CustomerId
JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id
WHERE customers.LastName = 'LOGAN' AND goods.Food = 'Croissant'

question: What are the receipt numbers for a customer with the last name Logan who purchased a croissant?
sql:
SELECT receipts.ReceiptNumber
FROM customers
JOIN receipts ON customers.Id = receipts.CustomerId
JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id
WHERE customers.LastName = 'Logan' AND goods.Food = 'croissant'

question: What is the receipt number and date of the receipt in which the most expensive item was bought?

SELECT receipts.ReceiptNumber, receipts.Date
FROM goods
JOIN items ON items.Item = goods.Id
JOIN receipts ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
ORDER BY goods.Price DESC
LIMIT 1

question: What is the receipt number and date corresponding to the receipt for which the most expensive item was purchased?

question: What is the item that was bought the least number of times?

FROM items
JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id
GROUP BY items.Item
ORDER BY COUNT(items.Item) ASC
LIMIT 1

question: Which item was bought the fewest times?

aggregations: COUNT(items.Item) AS purchase_count GROUP BY items.Item

sql:

FROM items
JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id

ORDER BY COUNT(items.Item) ASC
LIMIT 1

question: Give me the distinct id and price for all goods whose price is below the average of all goods?
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT goods.Id, goods.Price
FROM goods
WHERE goods.Price < (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM goods)

question: What are the distinct ids and prices for goods that cost less than the average good?

question: What are the distinct ids of all goods that are cheaper than some goods of type Tart?

sql:

FROM goods g
WHERE g.Food = 'Tart'
  AND g.Price < (SELECT MAX(g2.Price) FROM goods g2 WHERE g2.Food = 'Tart')

question: Give the distinct ids for goods that cost less than any Tart.

question: Find all receipts which has the latest date. Also tell me that date.

question: Find all receipts which either has the earliest date or has a good with price above 10.
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT receipts.ReceiptNumber, receipts.Date, goods.Price
FROM receipts
JOIN items ON items.Receipt = receipts.ReceiptNumber
JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id
WHERE receipts.Date = (SELECT MIN(Date) FROM receipts)
   OR goods.Price > 10

question: What are all the receipt numbers that have a good with a price above 10 or have the earliest date?

joins: receipts JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
items JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id

sql:
SELECT DISTINCT receipts.ReceiptNumber
FROM receipts
JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id
WHERE goods.Price > 10
   OR receipts.Date = (SELECT MIN(Date) FROM receipts)

question: What are highest, lowest, and average prices of goods, grouped and ordered by flavor?

question: Return the lowest and highest prices of goods grouped and ordered by food type.
sql:
SELECT MIN(goods.Price) AS min_price, MAX(goods.Price) AS max_price, goods.Food
FROM goods
GROUP BY goods.Food
ORDER BY goods.Food

question: What are the minimum and maximum prices of food goods, ordered by food?
sql:
SELECT MIN(goods.Price) AS min_price, MAX(goods.Price) AS max_price, goods.Food
FROM goods
GROUP BY goods.Food
ORDER BY goods.Food

question: Find the top three dates with the most receipts.

joins: items JOIN receipts ON items.Receipt = receipts.ReceiptNumber
aggregations: COUNT(items.Receipt) AS receipt_count GROUP BY receipts.Date

sql:
SELECT receipts.Date, COUNT(items.Receipt) AS receipt_count
FROM items
JOIN receipts ON items.Receipt = receipts.ReceiptNumber
GROUP BY receipts.Date
ORDER BY receipt_count DESC
LIMIT 3

question: What are the three dates for which the most receipts were given?
sql:
SELECT receipts.Date
FROM receipts
JOIN items ON items.Receipt = receipts.ReceiptNumber
GROUP BY receipts.Date
ORDER BY COUNT(items.Receipt) DESC
LIMIT 3

question: Which customer shopped most often? How many times?
sql:
SELECT customers.Id, customers.LastName, customers.FirstName, COUNT(receipts.ReceiptNumber) AS purchase_count
FROM customers
JOIN receipts ON customers.Id = receipts.CustomerId
JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
GROUP BY customers.Id, customers.FirstName, customers.LastName
ORDER BY purchase_count DESC
LIMIT 1

question: Give the customer id of the customer that made the most purchases, as well as the number of purchases made.

joins: receipts JOIN customers ON receipts.CustomerId = customers.Id
items JOIN receipts ON items.Receipt = receipts.ReceiptNumber

sql:
SELECT customers.Id, COUNT(items.Receipt) AS purchase_count
FROM customers
JOIN receipts ON receipts.CustomerId = customers.Id
JOIN items ON items.Receipt = receipts.ReceiptNumber
GROUP BY receipts.CustomerId
ORDER BY purchase_count DESC
LIMIT 1

question: For each date, return how many distinct customers visited on that day.
sql:
SELECT receipts.Date, COUNT(DISTINCT receipts.CustomerId) AS distinct_customers
FROM receipts
JOIN customers ON receipts.CustomerId = customers.Id
GROUP BY receipts.Date

question: How many cusomters visited on each date?
sql:
SELECT COUNT(receipts.CustomerId) AS customer_count, receipts.Date
FROM receipts
JOIN customers ON receipts.CustomerId = customers.Id
GROUP BY receipts.Date

question: Give me the first name and last name of customers who have bought apple flavor Tart.
sql:
SELECT customers.FirstName, customers.LastName
FROM customers
JOIN receipts ON customers.Id = receipts.CustomerId
JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id
WHERE goods.Flavor = 'apple' AND goods.Food = 'Tart'

question: What are the full names of customers who bought apple flavored Tarts?
sql:
SELECT customers.FirstName, customers.LastName
FROM customers
JOIN receipts ON customers.Id = receipts.CustomerId
JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id
WHERE goods.Flavor = 'apple' AND goods.Food = 'Tarts'

question: What are the ids of Cookies whose price is lower than any Croissant?

conditions: goods.Price < (SELECT MIN(goods.Price) FROM goods WHERE goods.Food = 'Croissant')

sql:
SELECT goods.Id
FROM goods
WHERE goods.Food = 'Cookie'
  AND goods.Price < (SELECT MIN(goods.Price) FROM goods WHERE goods.Food = 'Croissant')

question: Give the ids of cookes that are cheaper than any croissant.

conditions: goods.Food = 'cookie' AND goods.Price < (SELECT MIN(goods.Price) FROM goods WHERE goods.Food = 'croissant')

sql:
SELECT goods.Id
FROM goods
WHERE goods.Food = 'cookie' AND goods.Price < (SELECT MIN(goods.Price) FROM goods WHERE goods.Food = 'croissant')

question: Give me the ids of Cakes whose price is at least as much as the average price of Tart?

conditions: goods.Price >= (SELECT AVG(goods.Price) FROM goods WHERE goods.Food = 'Tart')

sql:
SELECT goods.Id
FROM goods
WHERE goods.Price >= (SELECT AVG(goods.Price) FROM goods WHERE goods.Food = 'Tart')

question: What are the ids of cakes that are at least as expensive as the average Tart?

conditions: goods.Price >= (SELECT AVG(goods.Price) FROM goods WHERE goods.Food = 'Tart')

sql:
SELECT goods.Id
FROM goods
WHERE goods.Price >= (SELECT AVG(goods.Price) FROM goods WHERE goods.Food = 'Tart')

question: What are the ids of goods whose price is above twice the average price of all goods?
sql:
SELECT goods.Id
FROM goods
WHERE goods.Price > 2 * (SELECT AVG(goods.Price) FROM goods)

question: Give the ids of goods that are more than twice as expensive as the average good.
sql:
SELECT goods.Id
FROM goods
WHERE goods.Price > (SELECT AVG(goods.Price) * 2 FROM goods)

question: List the id, flavor and type of food of goods ordered by price.
sql:
SELECT goods.Id, goods.Flavor, goods.Food
FROM goods
ORDER BY goods.Price

question: What are the ids, flavors, and food types of goods, ordered by price?
sql: SELECT goods.Id, goods.Flavor, goods.Food, goods.Price FROM goods ORDER BY goods.Price

question: Return a list of the id and flavor for Cakes ordered by flavor.
sql:
SELECT goods.Id, goods.Flavor
FROM goods
JOIN items ON items.Item = goods.Id
WHERE goods.Food = 'Cake'
ORDER BY goods.Flavor

question: What are the ids and flavors of cakes, ordered by flavor?
sql: SELECT goods.Id, goods.Flavor FROM goods ORDER BY goods.Flavor

question: Find all the items that have chocolate flavor but were not bought more than 10 times.

aggregations: COUNT(items.Item) AS item_count GROUP BY items.Item

sql:
SELECT goods.Flavor, goods.Id, items.Item
FROM goods
JOIN items ON items.Item = goods.Id
WHERE goods.Flavor = 'chocolate'
GROUP BY items.Item
HAVING COUNT(items.Item) <= 10

question: What are the items with chocolate flavor that were purchased at most 10 times.

aggregations: COUNT(items.Item) AS purchase_count GROUP BY items.Item

sql:
SELECT goods.Flavor, goods.Id, items.Item, COUNT(items.Item) AS purchase_count
FROM goods
JOIN items ON items.Item = goods.Id
WHERE goods.Flavor = 'chocolate'
GROUP BY items.Item
HAVING COUNT(items.Item) <= 10

question: What are the flavors available for Cake but not for Tart?
sql:
SELECT Flavor FROM goods WHERE Food = 'Cake'
EXCEPT
SELECT Flavor FROM goods WHERE Food = 'Tart'

question: Give the flavors of Cakes that are not available for Tart.

conditions: goods.Flavor NOT IN (SELECT g2.Flavor FROM goods g2 WHERE g2.Food = 'Tart')

sql: SELECT Flavor FROM goods WHERE Food = 'Cake' AND Flavor NOT IN (SELECT Flavor FROM goods WHERE Food = 'Tart')

question: What is the three most popular goods in this bakery?
sql:
SELECT goods.Id, goods.Food, COUNT(items.Item) AS popularity
FROM goods
JOIN items ON items.Item = goods.Id
JOIN receipts ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
GROUP BY goods.Id, goods.Food
ORDER BY popularity DESC
LIMIT 3

question: Give the three most purchased items at this bakery.
sql:
SELECT goods.Food, COUNT(items.Item) AS purchase_count
FROM goods
JOIN items ON items.Item = goods.Id
JOIN receipts ON items.Receipt = receipts.ReceiptNumber
GROUP BY goods.Food
ORDER BY purchase_count DESC
LIMIT 3

question: Find the ids of customers who have spent more than 150 dollars in total.
sql:
SELECT customers.Id
FROM customers
JOIN receipts ON customers.Id = receipts.CustomerId
JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id
GROUP BY customers.Id
HAVING SUM(goods.Price) > 150

question: What are the ids of customers who have spent over 150 dollars in total?
sql:
SELECT customers.Id
FROM customers
JOIN receipts ON customers.Id = receipts.CustomerId
JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id
GROUP BY customers.Id
HAVING SUM(goods.Price) > 150

question: Find the ids of customers whose average spending for each good is above 5.
sql:
SELECT customers.Id
FROM customers
JOIN receipts ON customers.Id = receipts.CustomerId
JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id
GROUP BY customers.Id, goods.Id
HAVING AVG(goods.Price) > 5

question: What are the ids of customers who spend more than 5 on average for each good?
sql:
SELECT customers.Id
FROM customers
JOIN receipts ON receipts.CustomerId = customers.Id
JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id
GROUP BY customers.Id, goods.Id
HAVING AVG(goods.Price) > 5

question: On which day did the bakery sell more than 100 dollars in total.
sql:
SELECT receipts.Date
FROM receipts
JOIN items ON items.Receipt = receipts.ReceiptNumber
JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id
GROUP BY receipts.Date
HAVING SUM(goods.Price) > 100

question: On what dates did the bakery sell more than 100 dollars worth of goods in total?

joins: receipts JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
items JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id

sql:
SELECT receipts.Date
FROM receipts
JOIN items ON receipts.ReceiptNumber = items.Receipt
JOIN goods ON items.Item = goods.Id
GROUP BY receipts.Date
HAVING SUM(goods.Price) > 100

question: How many drivers are there?
sql: SELECT COUNT(driver.Driver_ID) FROM driver

question: Find the total number of drivers.
sql: SELECT COUNT(driver.Driver_ID) FROM driver

question: Find the number of drivers whose points are greater than 150 for each make.

joins: driver JOIN team ON driver.Make = team.Make
aggregations: COUNT(*) AS driver_count GROUP BY driver.Make

sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS driver_count, driver.Make
FROM driver
JOIN team ON driver.Make = team.Make
WHERE CAST(driver.Points AS INTEGER) > 150
GROUP BY driver.Make

question: How many drivers receive points greater than 150 for each make? Show the make and the count.
sql:
SELECT driver.Make, COUNT(*) AS count
FROM driver
WHERE CAST(driver.Points AS NUMBER) > 150
GROUP BY driver.Make

question: Find the average age of drivers for each make.
sql:
SELECT AVG(driver.Age) AS average_age, driver.Make
FROM driver
JOIN team ON driver.Make = team.Make
GROUP BY driver.Make

question: What is the average age of drivers for each make? Return the average age and make.
sql:
SELECT AVG(driver.Age) AS average_age, driver.Make
FROM driver
JOIN team ON driver.Make = team.Make
GROUP BY driver.Make

question: What are the average laps of all the drivers who are younger than 20?
sql: SELECT AVG(Laps) FROM driver WHERE Age < 20

question: Compute the average laps of drivers under the age of 20.
sql: SELECT AVG(Laps) FROM driver WHERE Age < 20

question: What are the managers and sponsors of teams? Sort the results by Car Owners.
sql:
SELECT team.Manager, team.Sponsor, team.Car_Owner
FROM team
ORDER BY team.Car_Owner

question: Find the manager and sponsor for each team and order them by the car owner.
sql:
SELECT team.Manager, team.Sponsor, team.Car_Owner
FROM team
ORDER BY team.Car_Owner

question: Find the make that has more than one team.
sql:
SELECT team.Make
FROM team
GROUP BY team.Make
HAVING COUNT(team.Team_ID) > 1

question: Which make has more than one team?
sql: SELECT Make FROM team GROUP BY Make HAVING COUNT(Make) > 1

question: What are the makes of the teams with car owner "Buddy Arrington"?

question: Find the make of the team whose car owner is "Buddy Arrington".

question: What are the maximum and minimum points of drivers.

question: Find the highest and lowest points of drivers.

question: Count the number of drivers whose points are below 150.

question: List all the driver names in ascending order of age.

question: Sort the driver names by age in ascending order.

question: List all the driver names in descending order of points.

FROM driver

FROM driver

question: Show the maximum points of the drivers from countries with capital "Dublin"

FROM country 
JOIN driver ON driver.Country = country.Country_Id

WHERE country.Capital = 'Dublin'

question: What is the average age of drivers from countries with official native language "English"

FROM country 
JOIN driver ON driver.Country = country.Country_Id 
WHERE country.Official_native_language = 'English'

SELECT DISTINCT country.Country
FROM country

WHERE driver.Points > 150

FROM country
JOIN driver ON driver.Country = country.Country_Id

question: What is the capital of the country where the driver with the most points is from?

SELECT country.Capital
FROM driver
JOIN country ON driver.Country = country.Country_Id
ORDER BY CAST(driver.Points AS INTEGER) DESC
LIMIT 1

question: List each make with the number of drivers with that make.
sql: SELECT Make, COUNT(Driver_ID) AS count FROM driver GROUP BY Make

question: For each make, return the make and the count of drivers with that make.

aggregations: COUNT(*) AS driver_count GROUP BY driver.Make

question: List the make that are associated with most drivers.

joins: driver INNER JOIN team ON driver.Make = team.Make
aggregations: COUNT(driver.Driver_ID) AS driver_count GROUP BY driver.Make

sql:
SELECT driver.Make
FROM driver
INNER JOIN team ON driver.Make = team.Make
GROUP BY driver.Make
ORDER BY COUNT(driver.Driver_ID) DESC
LIMIT 1

question: Which make does the most drivers have?
sql: SELECT Make FROM driver GROUP BY Make ORDER BY COUNT(Make) DESC LIMIT 1

question: List the driver makes that are associated with at least three drivers.
sql:
SELECT driver.Make
FROM driver
GROUP BY driver.Make
HAVING COUNT(driver.Driver_ID) >= 3

question: Which make is associated with 3 or more drivers?

joins: driver INNER JOIN team ON driver.Make = team.Make

sql:
SELECT driver.Make
FROM driver
INNER JOIN team ON driver.Make = team.Make
GROUP BY driver.Make
HAVING COUNT(driver.Driver_ID) >= 3

question: List the names of teams that do not have any drivers.

joins: team LEFT JOIN team_driver ON team.Team_ID = team_driver.Team_ID

sql:
SELECT team.Team
FROM team
LEFT JOIN team_driver ON team.Team_ID = team_driver.Team_ID
WHERE team_driver.Driver_ID IS NULL

question: Which team does not have drivers?

joins: team LEFT JOIN team_driver ON team.Team_ID = team_driver.Team_ID

sql:
SELECT team.Team_ID, team.Team
FROM team
LEFT JOIN team_driver ON team.Team_ID = team_driver.Team_ID
WHERE team_driver.Driver_ID IS NULL

question: Which country has both drivers with make "Dodge" and drivers with make "Chevrolet"?
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT c.Country
FROM country c
JOIN driver d1 ON d1.Country = c.Country_Id
JOIN driver d2 ON d2.Country = c.Country_Id
WHERE d1.Make = 'Dodge' AND d2.Make = 'Chevrolet'

question: Find the countries in which there are both drivers with make "Dodge" and drivers with make "Chevrolet".
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT c.Country
FROM driver d1
JOIN country c ON d1.Country = c.Country_Id
WHERE d1.Make = 'Dodge'
AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM driver d2
    WHERE d2.Country = d1.Country
    AND d2.Make = 'Chevrolet'
)

question: Show total and average points of all drivers.
sql: SELECT SUM(CAST(driver.Points AS REAL)) AS total_points, AVG(CAST(driver.Points AS REAL)) AS average_points FROM driver

question: What are the total and average points of drivers?

question: Find the countries where no driver come from.

question: Which countries do not have any drivers?
sql:
SELECT country.Country

question: What are the manager and sponsor of the team that has the most drivers?
sql:
SELECT team.Manager, team.Sponsor
FROM team
JOIN team_driver ON team_driver.Team_ID = team.Team_ID
JOIN driver ON team_driver.Driver_ID = driver.Driver_ID
GROUP BY team.Team_ID
ORDER BY COUNT(driver.Driver_ID) DESC
LIMIT 1

question: Find the manager and sponsor of the team that has the most drivers.
sql:
SELECT team.Manager, team.Sponsor
FROM team
JOIN team_driver ON team_driver.Team_ID = team.Team_ID
JOIN driver ON team_driver.Driver_ID = driver.Driver_ID
GROUP BY team.Team_ID
ORDER BY COUNT(driver.Driver_ID) DESC
LIMIT 1

question: What are the manager and car owner of the team that has at least 2 drivers?

joins: team_driver JOIN team ON team_driver.Team_ID = team.Team_ID

sql:
SELECT team.Manager, team.Car_Owner
FROM team_driver
JOIN team ON team_driver.Team_ID = team.Team_ID
GROUP BY team.Team_ID
HAVING COUNT(team_driver.Driver_ID) >= 2

question: Find the team with two or more drivers and return the the manager and car owner of the team.

joins: team_driver JOIN team ON team_driver.Team_ID = team.Team_ID

sql:
SELECT team.Manager, team.Car_Owner
FROM team_driver
JOIN team ON team_driver.Team_ID = team.Team_ID
GROUP BY team.Team_ID
HAVING COUNT(team_driver.Driver_ID) >= 2

question: What is the average value of boxes for each warehouse?
sql:
SELECT AVG(Boxes.Value) AS average_value, Boxes.Warehouse
FROM Boxes
JOIN Warehouses ON Boxes.Warehouse = Warehouses.Code
GROUP BY Boxes.Warehouse

question: Find the average and total values of all boxes.
sql: SELECT AVG(Boxes.Value), SUM(Boxes.Value) FROM Boxes

question: What are the average and total values across all boxes?
sql: SELECT AVG(Boxes.Value), SUM(Boxes.Value) FROM Boxes

question: Find the average and total capacity of all warehouses.
sql: SELECT AVG(Capacity), SUM(Capacity) FROM Warehouses

question: What are the average and total capacities across all warehouses?
sql: SELECT AVG(Capacity), SUM(Capacity) FROM Warehouses

question: Find the average and maximum value for each different content.
sql:
SELECT AVG(Boxes.Value) AS avg_value, MAX(Boxes.Value) AS max_value, Boxes.Contents
FROM Boxes
GROUP BY Boxes.Contents

question: What are the average and maximum values for each type of content in boxes?
sql:
SELECT AVG(Boxes.Value) AS average_value, MAX(Boxes.Value) AS maximum_value, Boxes.Contents
FROM Boxes
GROUP BY Boxes.Contents

question: Find the content that has the highest total values in all boxes.

aggregations: SUM(Boxes.Value) AS total_value GROUP BY Boxes.Contents

sql:
SELECT Boxes.Contents, SUM(Boxes.Value) AS total_value
FROM Boxes
JOIN Warehouses ON Boxes.Warehouse = Warehouses.Code
GROUP BY Boxes.Contents
ORDER BY total_value DESC
LIMIT 1

question: What is the content with the greatest value across all boxes?
sql: SELECT Boxes.Value, Boxes.Contents FROM Boxes ORDER BY Boxes.Value DESC LIMIT 1

question: Select the average value of all the boxes.
sql: SELECT AVG(Boxes.Value) FROM Boxes

question: What is the average value of boxes?
sql: SELECT AVG(Boxes.Value) FROM Boxes

question: Select all distinct contents in all the boxes.
sql: SELECT DISTINCT Contents FROM Boxes

question: What are the different contents in boxes?
sql: SELECT DISTINCT Contents FROM Boxes

question: Find the number of all distinct contents in all the boxes.
sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Contents) FROM Boxes

question: How many different contents are stored in boxes?
sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Contents) FROM Boxes

question: Find all distinct locations of warehouses.
sql: SELECT DISTINCT Location FROM Warehouses

question: What are the different locations of warehouses?
sql: SELECT DISTINCT Location FROM Warehouses GROUP BY Location

question: Find the code of boxes that are stored at the warehouses located at Chicago or New York.
sql:
SELECT Boxes.Code
FROM Warehouses
JOIN Boxes ON Boxes.Warehouse = Warehouses.Code
WHERE Warehouses.Location = 'Chicago' OR Warehouses.Location = 'New York'

question: What are the codes of boxes stored in warehouses in either Chicago or New York?
sql:
SELECT Boxes.Code
FROM Warehouses
JOIN Boxes ON Boxes.Warehouse = Warehouses.Code
WHERE Warehouses.Location = 'Chicago' OR Warehouses.Location = 'New York'

question: Find the total value of boxes in the warehouses located at Chicago or New York.
sql:
SELECT SUM(Boxes.Value)
FROM Warehouses
JOIN Boxes ON Boxes.Warehouse = Warehouses.Code
WHERE Warehouses.Location = 'Chicago' OR Warehouses.Location = 'New York'

question: What is the total value of boxes located in Chicago or New York?
sql:
SELECT SUM(Boxes.Value)
FROM Warehouses
JOIN Boxes ON Boxes.Warehouse = Warehouses.Code
WHERE Warehouses.Location = 'Chicago' OR Warehouses.Location = 'New York'

question: Find all contents present in warehouses located in Chicago and those located in New York.
sql:
SELECT Warehouses.Location, Boxes.Contents
FROM Warehouses
JOIN Boxes ON Boxes.Warehouse = Warehouses.Code
WHERE Warehouses.Location = 'Chicago' OR Warehouses.Location = 'New York'

question: Find the contents that are stored in both Chicago and New York.
sql:
SELECT b1.Contents
FROM Boxes b1
JOIN Warehouses w1 ON b1.Warehouse = w1.Code
WHERE w1.Location = 'Chicago'
INTERSECT
SELECT b2.Contents
FROM Boxes b2
JOIN Warehouses w2 ON b2.Warehouse = w2.Code
WHERE w2.Location = 'New York'

question: Find the type of contents that are not in the warehouses located at New York.
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT Boxes.Contents
FROM Boxes
JOIN Warehouses ON Boxes.Warehouse = Warehouses.Code
WHERE Warehouses.Location != 'New York'

question: What types of contents cannot be found in warehouses in New York?
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT Boxes.Contents
FROM Warehouses
JOIN Boxes ON Boxes.Warehouse = Warehouses.Code
WHERE Warehouses.Location = 'New York'

question: Find the location of the warehouses which have contents Rocks but not Scissors.
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT w.Location
FROM Warehouses w
JOIN Boxes b ON w.Code = b.Warehouse
WHERE b.Contents = 'Rocks'
  AND b.Contents != 'Scissors'

question: What are the locations of warehouses that have boxes containing Rocks but not Scissors?

conditions: Boxes.Warehouse NOT IN (SELECT Boxes.Warehouse FROM Boxes WHERE Boxes.Contents = 'Scissors')

sql:
SELECT DISTINCT Warehouses.Location
FROM Warehouses
JOIN Boxes ON Warehouses.Code = Boxes.Warehouse
WHERE Boxes.Contents = 'Rocks'
  AND Boxes.Warehouse NOT IN (
    SELECT Boxes.Warehouse
    FROM Boxes
    WHERE Boxes.Contents = 'Scissors'
  )

question: Find the warehouses which store contents Rocks or Scissors.
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT w.Code, w.Location, w.Capacity
FROM Warehouses w
JOIN Boxes b ON b.Warehouse = w.Code
WHERE b.Contents = 'Rocks' OR b.Contents = 'Scissors'

question: What are the distinct warehouses that have boxes with Rocks or Scissors as contents?
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT Warehouses.Code
FROM Warehouses
JOIN Boxes ON Boxes.Warehouse = Warehouses.Code
WHERE Boxes.Contents = 'Rocks' OR Boxes.Contents = 'Scissors'

question: Find the location of the warehouses which store contents Rocks and Scissors.
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT w.Location
FROM Warehouses w
JOIN Boxes b ON w.Code = b.Warehouse
WHERE b.Contents = 'Rocks'
AND b.Contents = 'Scissors'

question: What are the locations of warehouses in which boxes that contain Rocks and Scissors are kept?
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT Warehouses.Location
FROM Warehouses
JOIN Boxes ON Warehouses.Code = Boxes.Warehouse
WHERE Boxes.Contents LIKE '%Rocks%' AND Boxes.Contents LIKE '%Scissors%'

question: List the code and contents of all boxes sorted by their values.
sql: SELECT Boxes.Code, Boxes.Contents, Boxes.Value FROM Boxes ORDER BY Boxes.Value

question: What are the codes and corresponding contents of all the boxes, ordered by their values?

SELECT Boxes.Code, Boxes.Contents, Boxes.Value
FROM Boxes
ORDER BY Boxes.Value

question: Find the code and contents of the box with the lowest value.
sql: SELECT Boxes.Code, Boxes.Contents FROM Boxes ORDER BY Boxes.Value ASC LIMIT 1

question: What is the code and contents for the box that has the smallest value?
sql: SELECT Boxes.Code, Boxes.Contents FROM Boxes ORDER BY Boxes.Value ASC LIMIT 1

question: Find the unique contents of all boxes whose value is higher than the average value of all boxes.
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT Boxes.Contents
FROM Boxes
WHERE Boxes.Value > (SELECT AVG(Boxes.Value) FROM Boxes)

question: What are the different contents of boxes for which the value is higher than the average value across all boxes?

SELECT DISTINCT Boxes.Contents
FROM Boxes
WHERE Boxes.Value > (SELECT AVG(Boxes.Value) FROM Boxes)

question: List all different types of contents ordered by contents.

question: What are the different contents of boxes, ordered alphabetically?

question: Find the code of all boxes whose value is higher than the value of any boxes with Rocks as content.

FROM Boxes

conditions: Boxes.Value > (SELECT MAX(Boxes.Value) FROM Boxes WHERE Boxes.Contents = 'Rocks')

sql:

FROM Boxes
JOIN Warehouses ON Boxes.Warehouse = Warehouses.Code
WHERE Boxes.Value > (SELECT MAX(Boxes.Value) FROM Boxes WHERE Boxes.Contents = 'Rocks')

question: What are the codes and corresponding contents of boxes for which their value is higher than the values of all boxes containing Scissors?

question: Find the total value of boxes stored in the warehouse with the largest capacity.

SELECT SUM(Boxes.Value) 
FROM Warehouses 
JOIN Boxes ON Boxes.Warehouse = Warehouses.Code 
ORDER BY Warehouses.Capacity DESC 
LIMIT 1

question: What is the total value of boxes kept in the warehouse with the greatest capacity?
sql:
SELECT SUM(Boxes.Value) 
FROM Warehouses 
JOIN Boxes ON Boxes.Warehouse = Warehouses.Code 
ORDER BY Warehouses.Capacity DESC

question: Select the warehouse code and the average value of the boxes only for those warehouses where the average value of the boxes is greater than 150.
sql:
SELECT Warehouses.Code, AVG(Boxes.Value) AS avg_value
FROM Warehouses
JOIN Boxes ON Boxes.Warehouse = Warehouses.Code
GROUP BY Warehouses.Code
HAVING AVG(Boxes.Value) > 150

question: What are the average values of boxes for each warehouse than has an average value greater than 150?
sql:
SELECT Boxes.Warehouse, AVG(Boxes.Value) AS avg_value
FROM Boxes
JOIN Warehouses ON Boxes.Warehouse = Warehouses.Code
GROUP BY Boxes.Warehouse
HAVING AVG(Boxes.Value) > 150

question: Find the total, average, and maximum capacity for different locations.
sql:
SELECT SUM(Warehouses.Capacity) AS total_capacity, AVG(Warehouses.Capacity) AS average_capacity, MAX(Warehouses.Capacity) AS max_capacity, Warehouses.Location
FROM Warehouses

question: For each location, what are the total, average, and maximum capacities of warehouses?
sql:
SELECT 
    Warehouses.Location, 
    SUM(Warehouses.Capacity) AS total_capacity, 
    AVG(Warehouses.Capacity) AS average_capacity, 
    MAX(Warehouses.Capacity) AS maximum_capacity
FROM 
    Warehouses
GROUP BY 
    Warehouses.Location
ORDER BY 
    Warehouses.Location

FROM Warehouses

HAVING COUNT(Boxes.Code) > Warehouses.Capacity

question: Show all home conferences.

question: Show all majors and major ranks for the university with name Augustana College.

joins: university JOIN major_ranking ON university.University_ID = major_ranking.University_ID
major_ranking JOIN major ON major_ranking.Major_ID = major.Major_ID

sql:
SELECT university.University_Name, major.Major_Name, major_ranking.Rank
FROM university
JOIN major_ranking ON university.University_ID = major_ranking.University_ID
JOIN major ON major_ranking.Major_ID = major.Major_ID
WHERE university.University_Name = 'Augustana College'

question: What are the ranks and names of all majors at Augustana College?

joins: major_ranking JOIN university ON major_ranking.University_ID = university.University_ID
major_ranking JOIN major ON major_ranking.Major_ID = major.Major_ID

sql:
SELECT major_ranking.Rank, major.Major_Name
FROM major_ranking
JOIN university ON major_ranking.University_ID = university.University_ID
JOIN major ON major_ranking.Major_ID = major.Major_ID
WHERE university.University_Name = 'Augustana College'
ORDER BY major_ranking.Rank

question: What is the name, city, state of the university with a rank 1 on Accounting major?

joins: university JOIN major_ranking ON university.University_ID = major_ranking.University_ID
major_ranking JOIN major ON major_ranking.Major_ID = major.Major_ID

sql:
SELECT university.University_Name, university.City, university.State
FROM university
JOIN major_ranking ON university.University_ID = major_ranking.University_ID
JOIN major ON major_ranking.Major_ID = major.Major_ID
WHERE major_ranking.Rank = 1 AND major.Major_Name = 'Accounting'

question: What is the name, city, and state of the university with number 1 ranked Accounting major?

FROM university
JOIN major_ranking ON major_ranking.University_ID = university.University_ID
JOIN major ON major_ranking.Major_ID = major.Major_ID

question: What is the name of the university that has most number of majors with rank 1?

sql:

FROM major_ranking
JOIN university ON major_ranking.University_ID = university.University_ID
WHERE major_ranking.Rank = 1
GROUP BY university.University_Name
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1

question: What is the name of the university with the most majors ranked number 1?

joins: university JOIN major_ranking ON university.University_ID = major_ranking.University_ID
major_ranking JOIN major ON major_ranking.Major_ID = major.Major_ID
aggregations: COUNT(major_ranking.Major_ID) AS top_major_count GROUP BY university.University_Name

FROM university

JOIN major ON major_ranking.Major_ID = major.Major_ID

question: Show all university names with both major Accounting and major Urban Education.

joins: university JOIN major_ranking ON university.University_ID = major_ranking.University_ID
major_ranking JOIN major ON major_ranking.Major_ID = major.Major_ID

sql:
SELECT DISTINCT u.University_Name
FROM university u
JOIN major_ranking mr ON u.University_ID = mr.University_ID
JOIN major m ON mr.Major_ID = m.Major_ID
WHERE m.Major_Name = 'Accounting'
AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM major_ranking mr2
    JOIN major m2 ON mr2.Major_ID = m2.Major_ID
    WHERE mr2.University_ID = u.University_ID
    AND m2.Major_Name = 'Urban Education'
)

question: What are the names of all universities that have both Accounting and Urban Education majors?

joins: university JOIN major_ranking ON university.University_ID = major_ranking.University_ID
major_ranking JOIN major ON major_ranking.Major_ID = major.Major_ID

sql:
SELECT DISTINCT u.University_Name
FROM university u
JOIN major_ranking mr1 ON u.University_ID = mr1.University_ID
JOIN major m1 ON mr1.Major_ID = m1.Major_ID
JOIN major_ranking mr2 ON u.University_ID = mr2.University_ID
JOIN major m2 ON mr2.Major_ID = m2.Major_ID
WHERE m1.Major_Name = 'Accounting' AND m2.Major_Name = 'Urban Education'

question: List all package sent or received by Leo Wong.

joins: Employee JOIN Shipment ON Employee.EmployeeID = Shipment.Manager
Shipment JOIN Package ON Shipment.ShipmentID = Package.Shipment
Package JOIN Client AS SenderClient ON Package.Sender = SenderClient.AccountNumber
Package JOIN Client AS RecipientClient ON Package.Recipient = RecipientClient.AccountNumber

sql:
SELECT Employee.Name, Shipment.ShipmentID, Package.PackageNumber, Package.Contents, Package.Weight, Package.Sender, Package.Recipient
FROM Employee
JOIN Shipment ON Employee.EmployeeID = Shipment.Manager
JOIN Package ON Shipment.ShipmentID = Package.Shipment
JOIN Client AS SenderClient ON Package.Sender = SenderClient.AccountNumber
JOIN Client AS RecipientClient ON Package.Recipient = RecipientClient.AccountNumber
WHERE Employee.Name = 'Leo Wong' OR SenderClient.Name = 'Leo Wong' OR RecipientClient.Name = 'Leo Wong'

question: Who sent most number of packages? List client name and number of packages sent by that client.

question: What is the smallest number of packages received and by whom ?

joins: Package JOIN Shipment ON Package.Shipment = Shipment.ShipmentID
Shipment JOIN Employee ON Shipment.Manager = Employee.EmployeeID

sql:
SELECT MIN(package_count) AS smallest_package_count, Employee.Name
FROM (
    SELECT COUNT(Package.PackageNumber) AS package_count, Employee.EmployeeID, Employee.Name
    FROM Package
    JOIN Shipment ON Package.Shipment = Shipment.ShipmentID
    JOIN Employee ON Shipment.Manager = Employee.EmployeeID
    GROUP BY Employee.EmployeeID, Employee.Name
) AS package_counts
ORDER BY package_count ASC
LIMIT 1

question: What are the 3 best selling books? Show their titles, author names, and press names.

question: Find the number of books that are sold more than 1000 for each publisher. List the press name as well.

aggregations: COUNT(*) AS book_count, press.Name GROUP BY press.Name

sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS book_count, press.Name
FROM press
JOIN book ON book.Press_ID = press.Press_ID
WHERE CAST(book.Sale_Amount AS INTEGER) > 1000
GROUP BY press.Name

question: For each press, return its name and the number of books that have sale amount above 1000.

aggregations: COUNT(book.Book_ID) AS book_count GROUP BY press.Name

sql:

FROM press
JOIN book ON book.Press_ID = press.Press_ID
WHERE CAST(book.Sale_Amount AS NUMBER) > 1000
GROUP BY press.Name

FROM Clients
JOIN Invoices ON Clients.client_id = Invoices.client_id
JOIN Meetings ON Clients.client_id = Meetings.client_id
GROUP BY Clients.client_id
HAVING COUNT(Meetings.meeting_id) = 1 AND Invoices.invoice_id IS NOT NULL

question: Return the ids, sic codes, and agency ids of clients who have attended 1 meeting and had an invoice.

joins: Clients JOIN Invoices ON Clients.client_id = Invoices.client_id
Clients JOIN Meetings ON Clients.client_id = Meetings.client_id
conditions: Meetings.meeting_id IN (SELECT meeting_id FROM Meetings GROUP BY client_id HAVING COUNT(meeting_id) = 1)

sql:
SELECT Clients.client_id, Clients.sic_code, Clients.agency_id
FROM Clients
JOIN Invoices ON Clients.client_id = Invoices.client_id
JOIN Meetings ON Clients.client_id = Meetings.client_id
WHERE Invoices.invoice_status IS NOT NULL
  AND Meetings.meeting_id IN (
    SELECT meeting_id
    FROM Meetings
    GROUP BY client_id
    HAVING COUNT(meeting_id) = 1
  )
GROUP BY Clients.client_id, Clients.sic_code, Clients.agency_id

question: List the id, first name and last name of the customers who both have placed more than 2 orders and have bought at least 3 items.

FROM Customers

JOIN Order_Items ON Orders.order_id = Order_Items.order_id
GROUP BY Customers.customer_id, Customers.customer_first_name, Customers.customer_last_name
HAVING COUNT(Orders.order_id) > 2 AND SUM(Order_Items.order_item_id) >= 3

question: Find the name and location of the stadiums which some concerts happened in the years of both 2014 and 2015.

joins: stadium INNER JOIN concert ON stadium.Stadium_ID = concert.Stadium_ID

sql:
SELECT DISTINCT s.Name, s.Location
FROM stadium s
INNER JOIN concert c ON s.Stadium_ID = c.Stadium_ID
WHERE c.Year = '2014'

    WHERE c2.Stadium_ID = s.Stadium_ID

)

question: How many models does each car maker produce? List maker full name, id and the number.
sql:
SELECT car_makers.FullName, car_makers.Id, COUNT(model_list.ModelId) AS model_count
FROM car_makers
JOIN model_list ON car_makers.Id = model_list.Maker
GROUP BY car_makers.FullName, car_makers.Id

question: Which model of the car has the minimum horsepower?
sql:
SELECT model_list.Model
FROM cars_data
JOIN car_names ON cars_data.Id = car_names.MakeId
JOIN model_list ON car_names.Model = model_list.Model
JOIN car_makers ON model_list.Maker = car_makers.Id
WHERE cars_data.Horsepower IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY CAST(cars_data.Horsepower AS INTEGER) ASC
LIMIT 1

SELECT countries.CountryId, countries.CountryName
FROM countries
JOIN car_makers ON countries.CountryId = car_makers.Country
JOIN model_list ON car_makers.Id = model_list.Maker
WHERE model_list.Model = 'fiat'
GROUP BY countries.CountryId, countries.CountryName
HAVING COUNT(car_makers.Id) > 3

question: What are the ids and names of all countries that either have more than 3 car makers or produce fiat model ?

joins: countries JOIN car_makers ON countries.CountryId = car_makers.Country
car_makers JOIN model_list ON car_makers.Id = model_list.Maker

sql:
SELECT DISTINCT countries.CountryId, countries.CountryName
FROM countries
JOIN car_makers ON countries.CountryId = car_makers.Country
JOIN model_list ON car_makers.Id = model_list.Maker
WHERE model_list.Model = 'fiat'
GROUP BY countries.CountryId, countries.CountryName
HAVING COUNT(car_makers.Id) > 3

question: Find all airlines that have flights from both airports 'APG' and 'CVO'.
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT a.Airline
FROM airlines a
JOIN flights f1 ON a.uid = f1.Airline
JOIN flights f2 ON a.uid = f2.Airline
WHERE f1.SourceAirport = 'APG' AND f2.SourceAirport = 'CVO'

question: Which airlines have departing flights from both APG and CVO airports?
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT a.Airline
FROM airlines a
JOIN flights f ON a.uid = f.Airline
WHERE f.SourceAirport = 'APG'
INTERSECT
SELECT DISTINCT a.Airline
FROM airlines a
JOIN flights f ON a.uid = f.Airline
WHERE f.SourceAirport = 'CVO'

question: Find all airlines that have flights from airport 'CVO' but not from 'APG'.
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT airlines.Airline
FROM airlines
JOIN flights ON airlines.uid = flights.Airline
JOIN airports ON flights.SourceAirport = airports.AirportCode
WHERE flights.SourceAirport = 'CVO'
AND flights.SourceAirport != 'APG'

question: Which airlines have departures from CVO but not from APG airports?
sql:
SELECT DISTINCT a.Airline
FROM airlines a
JOIN flights f ON f.Airline = a.uid
JOIN airports ap ON f.SourceAirport = ap.AirportCode
WHERE ap.AirportCode = 'CVO'
AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM flights f2
    JOIN airports ap2 ON f2.SourceAirport = ap2.AirportCode
    WHERE ap2.AirportCode = 'APG'
    AND f2.Airline = a.uid
)

question: Find the name of airports which do not have any flight in and out.

joins: airports LEFT JOIN flights ON airports.AirportCode = flights.SourceAirport OR airports.AirportCode = flights.DestAirport

sql:

FROM airports
LEFT JOIN flights ON airports.AirportCode = flights.SourceAirport OR airports.AirportCode = flights.DestAirport
WHERE flights.SourceAirport IS NULL AND flights.DestAirport IS NULL

SELECT Documents.Document_ID, Documents.Template_ID, Documents.Document_Description
FROM Documents
JOIN Templates ON Documents.Template_ID = Templates.Template_ID
WHERE Documents.Document_Name = 'Robbin CV'

question: How many documents are using the template with type code 'PPT'?

joins: Ref_Template_Types JOIN Templates ON Ref_Template_Types.Template_Type_Code = Templates.Template_Type_Code
Templates JOIN Documents ON Templates.Template_ID = Documents.Template_ID

sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM Ref_Template_Types 
JOIN Templates ON Ref_Template_Types.Template_Type_Code = Templates.Template_Type_Code 
JOIN Documents ON Templates.Template_ID = Documents.Template_ID 
WHERE Ref_Template_Types.Template_Type_Code = 'PPT'

Templates JOIN Documents ON Templates.Template_ID = Documents.Template_ID

question: Show all template ids and number of documents using each template.

joins: Templates INNER JOIN Documents ON Templates.Template_ID = Documents.Template_ID

sql:
SELECT Templates.Template_ID, COUNT(Documents.Document_ID) 
FROM Templates 
INNER JOIN Documents ON Templates.Template_ID = Documents.Template_ID 
GROUP BY Templates.Template_ID

question: What are all different template ids used for documents, and how many times were each of them used?

joins: Templates INNER JOIN Documents ON Templates.Template_ID = Documents.Template_ID

sql:
SELECT Templates.Template_ID, COUNT(Documents.Template_ID) AS usage_count
FROM Templates
INNER JOIN Documents ON Templates.Template_ID = Documents.Template_ID
GROUP BY Templates.Template_ID

question: What is the id and type code for the template used by the most documents?

joins: Documents JOIN Templates ON Documents.Template_ID = Templates.Template_ID

sql:
SELECT Templates.Template_ID, Templates.Template_Type_Code
FROM Documents
JOIN Templates ON Documents.Template_ID = Templates.Template_ID
GROUP BY Templates.Template_ID, Templates.Template_Type_Code
ORDER BY COUNT(Documents.Document_ID) DESC
LIMIT 1

question: Return the id and type code of the template that is used for the greatest number of documents.

joins: Templates JOIN Documents ON Templates.Template_ID = Documents.Template_ID

sql:
SELECT Templates.Template_ID, Templates.Template_Type_Code
FROM Templates
JOIN Documents ON Templates.Template_ID = Documents.Template_ID
GROUP BY Templates.Template_ID, Templates.Template_Type_Code
ORDER BY COUNT(Documents.Document_ID) DESC
LIMIT 1

question: Show ids for all templates that are used by more than one document.

joins: Templates JOIN Documents ON Templates.Template_ID = Documents.Template_ID

sql:
SELECT Templates.Template_ID
FROM Templates
JOIN Documents ON Templates.Template_ID = Documents.Template_ID
GROUP BY Templates.Template_ID
HAVING COUNT(Documents.Document_ID) > 1

question: What are the template ids of any templates used in more than a single document?

GROUP BY Templates.Template_ID

question: What the smallest version number and its template type code?
sql:
SELECT MIN(Templates.Version_Number) AS Version_Number, Templates.Template_Type_Code
FROM Templates
GROUP BY Templates.Template_Type_Code
ORDER BY Version_Number ASC
LIMIT 1

question: Return the lowest version number, along with its corresponding template type code.

joins: Templates JOIN Ref_Template_Types ON Templates.Template_Type_Code = Ref_Template_Types.Template_Type_Code

sql:
SELECT MIN(Templates.Version_Number) AS min_version, Ref_Template_Types.Template_Type_Code
FROM Templates
JOIN Ref_Template_Types ON Templates.Template_Type_Code = Ref_Template_Types.Template_Type_Code
GROUP BY Ref_Template_Types.Template_Type_Code
ORDER BY MIN(Templates.Version_Number) ASC
LIMIT 1

question: Show all template type codes that are not used by any document.

joins: Ref_Template_Types JOIN Templates ON Ref_Template_Types.Template_Type_Code = Templates.Template_Type_Code
Templates JOIN Documents ON Templates.Template_ID = Documents.Template_ID

sql:
SELECT Template_Type_Code 
FROM Ref_Template_Types 
WHERE Template_Type_Code NOT IN (
    SELECT DISTINCT Templates.Template_Type_Code 
    FROM Templates 
    JOIN Documents ON Templates.Template_ID = Documents.Template_ID
)

SELECT Template_Type_Code 
FROM Ref_Template_Types 
WHERE Template_Type_Code NOT IN (
    SELECT DISTINCT t.Template_Type_Code 
    FROM Templates t 
    JOIN Documents d ON t.Template_ID = d.Template_ID
)
